rule: raw:design-strategy-strategy-exploration-omega-cycle (hive raw 47) + raw:design-strategy-falsifier-retire-rule (hive raw 71, report-only) + raw:design-strategy-honesty-admissibility (hive raw 73) + raw:design-strategy-audit-append-only (hive raw 77) + raw:design-strategy-sentinel-update (hive raw 80) + raw:design-strategy-ai-native-error-message (hive raw 66)
purpose: Close the final R5 defense gap. Registry + bridge files have per-file SHA256 tracking with hash-chained rotation ledgers, but atlas data files (n6/atlas.n6 + 9 active append shards) have no file-SHA tracking. A bulk silent edit to atlas.n6 touching values that no falsifier covers would slip past every existing defense. This Ω-cycle establishes per-shard SHA256 baseline (state/atlas_sha256.tsv), drift detector (atlas_health.sh), R5 chain rotator (atlas_sha256_rotate.sh + state/atlas_sha256_rotation_log.jsonl), and weaves both into the existing aggregator surfaces.
trigger: user 2026-04-26 prompt: 'Ω-cycle (오메가 사이클) — extend R5 file-SHA tracking from registry+bridges to atlas data files themselves (CLOSE final defense gap)'

out_of_scope: n6/atlas.millennium.n6 (not part of atlas.n6 + atlas.append.* glob — separate millennium-problem ledger)
n6/atlas.signals.n6 (not part of atlas.n6 + atlas.append.* glob — separate signals ledger)
n6/_retired/atlas.append.chip-p5-2.n6 (retired before Ω-cycle, not in glob)

constraint_compliance.raw_66_reason_fix: atlas_health TAMPERED line emits reason=atlas_sha256_mismatch + fix=audit_git_log_or_rotate_baseline + declared/live hashes; missing-shard line emits reason=shard_missing + fix=restore_from_git_or_remove_baseline_row; atlas_sha256_rotate ERROR line emits reason+fix in stderr
constraint_compliance.raw_71_report_only: atlas_health REPORTS only — never modifies n6/atlas*.n6 contents; atlas_sha256_rotate ROTATES baseline TSV + appends ledger only — never modifies n6/atlas*.n6 contents either
constraint_compliance.raw_73_admissibility: all classification deltas backed by real bash exec results captured in mutation_cycle below — no fabrication; --reason field on rotate entries records why rotation occurred
constraint_compliance.raw_77_audit_append_only: additive patches — no existing logic deleted in ledger_verify.sh (only new 'atlas' case added in switch); no existing logic deleted in atlas_status_all.sh or health_check_all.sh (only new R5 atlas blocks appended); baseline TSV created additively at state/atlas_sha256.tsv
constraint_compliance.raw_80_sentinel: __ATLAS_HEALTH__ <PASS|WARN|FAIL> total=N pass=P tampered=T duration_ms=DD (new surface — no prior version to break); __ATLAS_SHA256_ROTATE__ <ROTATED|SKIPPED|ERROR> per-shard + __ATLAS_ROTATE__ aggregate (new surface); __HEALTH_CHECK_ALL__ extended with atlas=P/T + atlas_ledger=<status>(N) fields (backward compatible — older sed scripts ignore extra fields); __ATLAS_STATUS_ALL__ extended with defense_r5_atlas_ledger + defense_r5_atlas_health + atlas_pass + atlas_tampered (backward compatible)

axes_surfaced.name: atlas_data_file_inventory
axes_surfaced.rationale: Main + 9 append shards form the atlas substrate. atlas.millennium.n6 + atlas.signals.n6 are intentionally excluded (separate ledgers — not appended via atlas.append.* convention). Retired chip-p5-2 lives in n6/_retired/ and is therefore not in the active glob.

axes_surfaced.name: per_shard_sha256_baseline
axes_surfaced.method: python3 hashlib.sha256 over raw bytes of each n6 file, truncated to 16 hex chars (matches falsifier sha256_16 + bridge sha256_16 helper convention)
axes_surfaced.git_visibility: state/* ignored per existing precedent (matches falsifier_registry.sha256 + bridge_sha256.tsv)

axes_surfaced.name: drift_detector
axes_surfaced.shape: mirrors bridge_health.sh: --quiet | --json | --strict | --help; iterate TSV; sha256_file_16 helper; PASS/TAMPERED per shard; aggregate sentinel + JSONL append to state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl (shared timeline)
axes_surfaced.differences_from_bridge_health: no per-entry network selftest (atlas shards are inert data, not executable bridges) — only SHA verification + missing-file detection. No OFFLINE-FALLBACK counter (n/a). missing counter added (TAMPERED-class) for shards that drop off disk.

axes_surfaced.name: r5_rotator
axes_surfaced.shape: mirrors bridge_sha256_rotate.sh: default scan+rotate | --dry-run | --shard PATH | --reason TEXT | --quiet | --json
axes_surfaced.ledger_format: per line: {ts, shard, old_sha, new_sha, old_lines, new_lines, trigger, reason, prev_hash}
axes_surfaced.chain_pattern: R5 OPT-D — prev_hash = sha256(prev_line) | 'genesis' for first entry
axes_surfaced.tsv_rotation_columns: col 2 sha256_16 + col 3 lines + col 4 last_verified_utc (line count tracked alongside SHA for forensic context)

axes_surfaced.name: ledger_verify_atlas_shortcut
axes_surfaced.patch: switch case extended with `atlas) LOG=state/atlas_sha256_rotation_log.jsonl` between the existing bridge and falsifier cases; help-text + error-trailer fix lists updated to include atlas
axes_surfaced.back_compat: no existing case touched; pre-R5 grandfathering still applies; --ledger PATH absolute/relative still works; default ledger unchanged (falsifier registry)
axes_surfaced.verify_run: ledger_verify --ledger atlas → __LEDGER_VERIFY__ EMPTY entries=0 broken_at=none ledger=atlas_sha256_rotation_log.jsonl

axes_surfaced.name: atlas_status_all_and_health_check_all
atlas_status_all_new_blocks: two new human-readable echo lines under defense block
two new sentinel fields: defense_r5_atlas_ledger=<status>(<N>) defense_r5_atlas_health=<status> atlas_pass=<P/T> atlas_tampered=<N>
EXIT_CODE escalation if atlas_ledger=FAIL or atlas_health=FAIL
health_check_all_new_blocks: TAMP_TOTAL recomputed as F_TAMP + B_TAMP + A_TAMP
compact line extended: 'F: ... | B: ... | A: P/T STATUS | tampered=N (F=Ft B=Bt A=At) | S: ... | L: ... | BL: ... | AL: ...'
fix-trailer reasons extended with atlas_health + atlas_ledger remediation hints
axes_surfaced.verify_run_health_check_all: __HEALTH_CHECK_ALL__ FAIL falsifier=103/105 bridge=16/16 atlas=10/10 tampered=0 status=ok ledger=EMPTY(0) bridge_ledger=PASS(2) atlas_ledger=EMPTY(0) (FAIL is from pre-existing falsifier subcheck 103/105, not from new atlas wiring — atlas itself is 10/10 PASS tampered=0)

axes_surfaced.name: end_to_end_validation
axes_surfaced.baseline_run: bash tool/atlas_health.sh → __ATLAS_HEALTH__ PASS total=10 pass=10 tampered=0
axes_surfaced.detection_run: bash tool/atlas_health.sh → 'atlas.append.hexa-sim-bridges.n6  TAMPERED   declared=c0db6345274ed5f3  live=a4d36a15548cbbe0  reason=atlas_sha256_mismatch  fix=audit_git_log_or_rotate_baseline' + __ATLAS_HEALTH__ FAIL total=10 pass=9 tampered=1
axes_surfaced.rotate_dry_run_during_mutation: bash tool/atlas_sha256_rotate.sh --dry-run → '__ATLAS_SHA256_ROTATE__ SKIPPED shard=atlas.append.hexa-sim-bridges.n6 old=c0db6345274ed5f3 new=a4d36a15548cbbe0' + __ATLAS_ROTATE__ PASS scanned=10 rotated=1 dry_run=1; TSV unchanged (verified post-restore)
axes_surfaced.post_restore_run: bash tool/atlas_health.sh --quiet → __ATLAS_HEALTH__ PASS total=10 pass=10 tampered=0 (clean)
axes_surfaced.tsv_integrity_check: grep hexa-sim-bridges atlas_sha256.tsv → row preserved (sha c0db6345274ed5f3, lines 185, ts 2026-04-25T23:58:55Z) — dry-run did not mutate TSV

tier_1_artifacts: tool/atlas_health.sh (164 lines, new — drift detector for 10 atlas shards)
tool/atlas_sha256_rotate.sh (233 lines, new — R5 OPT-D rotator + chain ledger)
tool/atlas_status_all.sh (+30 lines wiring R5 atlas ledger + atlas_health into defense block + sentinel)
tool/health_check_all.sh (+34 lines: 6th + 7th subchecks atlas_health + atlas_ledger; wrapper sentinel extended)
state/atlas_sha256.tsv (15 lines, 1329 bytes — per-shard SHA256 baseline)

atlas.r5_chain_ledger: state/atlas_sha256_rotation_log.jsonl (EMPTY, ready for first legitimate rotation — NEW)

evidence: all 10 shards baselined and verified PASS
mutation cycle: TAMPERED detected on hexa-sim-bridges shard, restoration verified clean
dry-run rotate detects drift without mutating TSV/ledger
ledger_verify --ledger atlas accepted; EMPTY status on fresh ledger
both aggregators (atlas_status_all + health_check_all) emit new R5 atlas fields
defense parity: registry + bridges + atlas all carry R1 per-entry SHA + R5 chain ledger + ledger_verify shortcut
fixpoint_marker.next_natural_step: first legitimate atlas rotation will populate state/atlas_sha256_rotation_log.jsonl with chain root (prev_hash=genesis); subsequent rotations chain via prev_hash = sha256(prev_line); ledger_verify --ledger atlas will switch from EMPTY to PASS once entries land

out_of_band_observations: atlas.millennium.n6 (490 lines) and atlas.signals.n6 (4451 lines) sit alongside but are out of scope for this Ω-cycle (different ledger lineage). Future Ω-cycle could extend atlas_sha256.tsv format to optionally include them, or create separate millennium_sha256.tsv / signals_sha256.tsv if they accrue independent rotation cadences.

ledger_verify default lookup uses git rev-parse —show-toplevel which from cwd=hive points to /Users/ghost/core/hive — recommendation: invoke ledger_verify with explicit NEXUS_ROOT=$HOME/core/nexus from cron or external invokers, or add a --root flag in a follow-up Ω-cycle. Within atlas_status_all + health_check_all this is already handled (both pass NEXUS_ROOT through env).
